evidence: we continue to invest in new capabilities and the use of emerging technologies, including investments in AI, across various aspects of our business
leveraging artificial intelligence (AI) across the marketing value chain to enhance personalization at scale, increase speed to market and reduce costs
using AI to identify insights to inform consumer-centric campaigns, develop and test concepts, and produce creative content
using AI solutions, including machine learning and generative AI tools, to assist in the development of our products, engage with consumers, and in the use of internal tools
strategic utilization of data to provide better visibility into consumer trends, to increase responsiveness in our product development
risks related to harmful content, inaccuracies, hallucinations, bias or discrimination, and intellectual property infringement
use of AI may increase cybersecurity and data privacy risks, such as intended, unintended, or inadvertent access to, transmission, or leakage of proprietary or sensitive information
obligation to comply with the evolving regulatory landscape could entail significant costs, negatively affect our business, or limit our ability to incorporate certain AI capabilities

limitations: No quantified dollar amounts or headcount figures tied to AI investment were present in the excerpts; investment intensity capped at 3.

AI narrative describes named deployed use cases (marketing, product development, consumer engagement) but no quantified business results (e.g., % cost savings, revenue lift) were disclosed, capping concreteness at 4.

Risk disclosure describes specific risk mechanisms (hallucinations, data leakage, regulatory compliance costs) with operational detail but no quantified risk exposure or named governance/oversight bodies, capping risk depth at 4.
